No Poverty.reason: The course focuses on language and writing skills, with no explicit or inferable connection to poverty eradication, economic resources for the poor, or social protection systems.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content is entirely focused on language acquisition and academic writing, with no mention of food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The syllabus does not contain any information related to health services, disease prevention, mental health, or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3). It develops academic and professional writing skills (Target 4.4) and fosters cultural knowledge and an international perspective (Target 4.7).
evidence: This course is designed to equip students’ academic writing skills
To possess basic language proficiency and application skills
Acquire cultural knowledge through the use of Vietnamese language

Gender Equality.reason: The syllabus does not mention gender-specific issues, discrimination, violence, or empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content is unrelated to water resources, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no mention of energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency in the course description.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops professional communication skills and provides vocabulary in economics, foundational for obtaining decent work and participating in economic life (Target 8.5).
evidence: exchange information, ideas, plans, knowledge and experiences in different professional areas
Obtain extended vocabulary used in academic learning, culture and society, politics and economics

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The syllabus does not cover topics related to infrastructure, industrialization, innovation, or technological capabilities.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course promotes intercultural understanding, an international perspective, and language skills, which can contribute to social inclusion (Target 10.2) and facilitate mobility (Target 10.7).
evidence: Be able to deal with difficult situations occurred in areas where spoken language is Vietnamese

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The acquisition of cultural knowledge through language learning can minimally contribute to the appreciation and safeguarding of cultural heritage (Target 11.4).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course does not address sustainable consumption, resource management, waste reduction, or related topics.

Climate Action.reason: There is no content in the syllabus related to climate change, its impacts, or mitigation/adaptation strategies.

Life Below Water.reason: The course content is unrelated to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: The syllabus does not cover terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course teaches argumentative writing and fosters an international perspective, which can minimally support participatory decision-making and peaceful dialogue (Target 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course develops language proficiency and an international perspective, essential for building international partnerships, facilitating knowledge sharing (Target 17.6), and enhancing global cooperation (Target 17.16).
evidence: exchange information, ideas, plans, knowledge and experiences in different professional areas
